implementation_groups.name: Group A — Layout Shell
implementation_groups.description: Sticky navbar with mobile sheet menu, language switcher (FR/NL/EN), and 4-column footer. These are rendered in the root layout and shared across all pages.

implementation_groups.name: Group B — Shared Sections & UI Primitives
implementation_groups.description: Reusable hero variants (split and centered, parameterized via props/i18n keys), CTA banner, and shared UI primitives (section wrapper with consistent spacing, heading component, scroll-reveal animation wrapper, font configuration).

implementation_groups.name: Group C — Home Page Sections
implementation_groups.description: All home-specific section components: 4-card service grid, team preview (founder + practitioners), testimonial carousel (3 cards), FAQ accordion (6 items), and contact info block (address, phone, email, map embed, hours). Includes corresponding data files.

implementation_groups.name: Group D — Inner Page Sections
implementation_groups.description: Sections unique to inner pages: alternating-row service details (Services page), full team profiles with bios (About page), 4-step how-it-works process (About page), and contact form with embedded map (Contact page).

decisions.decision: Hero components are parameterized, not duplicated per page
decisions.rationale: hero-split is used on Home and About, hero-centered on Services and Contact. Each accepts i18n keys and optional props (image, layout flip) rather than creating 4 separate hero files. This reduces duplication while keeping each variant a single file.

decisions.decision: CTA banner is a single shared component
decisions.rationale: Used on Home and Services pages with identical layout but different copy. Translation keys differentiate per-page usage. Listed in shared_dependencies so both Group C and Group D can import it without ownership conflict.

decisions.decision: Data files are co-located with the group that primarily authors them
decisions.rationale: services.ts and team.ts live in Group C (home sections) because that group creates the initial data structures. Group D reuses them via import. contact.ts is in Group C since the home contact-info section is the primary consumer; the contact page form-map reuses it.

decisions.decision: Separate mobile-nav component extracted from navbar
decisions.rationale: The mobile sheet menu has enough complexity (animation, overlay, accordion sub-items, language switcher) to warrant its own file. Keeps navbar.tsx focused on the desktop sticky bar. Both are in Group A.

decisions.decision: UI primitives (section-wrapper, section-heading, animated-element) in Group B
decisions.rationale: These are consumed by every section across all groups. Placing them in Group B (shared sections) means they are built first alongside the hero components, which are also the first sections visible on every page. Groups C and D depend on Group B completing first.

decisions.decision: i18n setup files excluded from implementation groups
decisions.rationale: Per the rules, next-intl configuration (middleware.ts, i18n/request.ts, i18n/routing.ts, i18n/navigation.ts, messages/*.json) is handled during scaffold phase, not by frontend-engineer agents. These files appear in component_tree for completeness but are not assigned to any group.

decisions.decision: Page files excluded from implementation groups
decisions.rationale: Page assembly (src/app/[locale]/**/page.tsx and layout.tsx) is handled by the orchestrator in Wave D after all section components are built. Pages simply compose sections in order.
The design spec explicitly sets pricing to null. Healthcare practices in Belgium typically avoid public pricing due to variable insurance coverage.
